data.title: TG THERAPEUTICS, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
TG Therapeutics, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company focused on the development and commercialization of novel treatments for B-cell diseases, has released its latest Form 10-Q report. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational advancements, driven primarily by the success of its flagship product, BRIUMVI. Below is a detailed summary of the company's financial and business performance for the recent period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $141.1 million, reflecting a significant increase from $73.5 million in the prior year period, driven by greater market penetration of BRIUMVI.
  • Operating Income: $34.8 million, up from $8.8 million, indicating improved operational efficiency and cost management.
  • Net Income: $28.2 million, compared to $6.9 million, showcasing strong financial performance and profitability.
  • Net Income Per Share - Basic: $0.19, up from $0.05, reflecting the company's improved earnings per share.
  • Net Income Per Share - Diluted: $0.17, compared to $0.04, indicating enhanced shareholder value.

Business Highlights

  • Product Revenue Segments: The company's product revenue is primarily derived from the sales of BRIUMVI, an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody, in the United States. The increase in product revenue is attributed to greater market penetration.
  • Geographical Performance: BRIUMVI has been approved and launched in several regions outside the United States, including the European Union, United Kingdom, Switzerland, and Australia. The company has partnered with Neuraxpharm for commercialization in these regions, which has contributed to the revenue from license, milestone, and royalty agreements.
  • New Product Launches: The company has initiated a Phase 1 clinical trial for subcutaneous BRIUMVI in patients with RMS and announced plans for a Phase 3 pivotal program to evaluate different dosing schedules. Additionally, the company has begun a clinical trial for ublituximab in patients with myasthenia gravis.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue expanding the market presence of BRIUMVI and is actively pursuing the development of its pipeline, including the progression of azer-cel, an allogeneic CAR T cell therapy for autoimmune diseases. The company is also focused on achieving additional regulatory and commercial milestones with its partners.

SEC Filing: TG THERAPEUTICS, INC. [ TGTX ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: REDWOOD TRUST INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Redwood Trust Inc., a prominent player in the real estate investment trust (REIT) sector, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, reflecting both challenges and strategic realignments in its business segments.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest Income: $284.3 million, reflecting an increase from $225.4 million in the prior year period, driven by higher interest income from residential consumer loans.

Net Interest Income: $13.8 million, a decrease from $25.3 million in the prior year period, primarily due to increased interest expense on asset-backed securities.

Net (Loss) Income: $(98.5) million, compared to a net income of $15.5 million in the prior year period, impacted by significant investment fair value changes and higher general and administrative expenses.

Net (Loss) Income Available to Common Stockholders: $(100.2) million, reflecting a substantial decline from $13.8 million in the prior year period.

Basic (Loss) Earnings Per Common Share: $(0.76), compared to $0.10 in the prior year period, indicating a significant decrease in earnings per share.

Diluted (Loss) Earnings Per Common Share: $(0.76), consistent with the basic earnings per share, highlighting the impact of the net loss on shareholder value.

Business Highlights

Segment Performance: Redwood Trust operates in four segments: Sequoia Mortgage Banking, CoreVest Mortgage Banking, Redwood Investments, and Legacy Investments. The Legacy Investments segment was newly established to manage assets not aligned with core strategic objectives.

Sequoia Mortgage Banking: This segment focuses on residential consumer loans, with significant activity in loan acquisitions and sales. The segment reported net interest income and non-interest income from mortgage banking activities.

CoreVest Mortgage Banking: This segment deals with residential investor loans, including term and bridge loans. It reported gains from mortgage banking activities and managed a portfolio of loans with varying interest rates and maturities.

Redwood Investments: This segment includes investments in securities and other financial instruments. It experienced fair value changes and managed a diverse portfolio of assets.

Legacy Investments: This new segment includes legacy unsecuritized bridge and term loan portfolios and other non-core assets. It is focused on strategic realignment and disposition of these assets.

Loan Modifications: The company engaged in loan modifications, particularly for bridge loans, to address project delays and borrower financial stress. These modifications included interest rate adjustments and maturity extensions.

Future Outlook: Redwood Trust is focused on strategic realignment, particularly within the Legacy Investments segment, to optimize capital deployment and align with core business strategies.

SEC Filing: REDWOOD TRUST INC [ RWT ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Krispy Kreme, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Krispy Kreme, Inc., a renowned global doughnut company, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal 2025. The report reveals significant financial and operational developments, reflecting the company's strategic decisions and market conditions.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenues: $379.8 million, reflecting a decrease of 13.5% compared to the same quarter last year, driven by a decline in product sales and royalties.

Operating (Loss)/Income: $(434.6) million, a significant decline from an income of $6.9 million in the prior year, primarily due to goodwill and other asset impairments.

Net Loss: $(441.1) million, compared to a net loss of $(4.9) million in the previous year, largely impacted by impairment charges and the termination of the McDonald's USA agreement.

Net Loss Attributable to Krispy Kreme, Inc.: $(435.3) million, compared to $(5.5) million in the prior year, reflecting the impact of strategic decisions and market conditions.

Net Loss Per Share - Basic and Diluted: $(2.55), compared to $(0.03) in the previous year, indicating a substantial increase in losses per share due to the factors mentioned above.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The U.S. segment reported net revenues of $230.1 million for the quarter, a decrease from $289.3 million in the same period last year. The International segment generated $132.8 million, slightly up from $125.3 million, while the Market Development segment saw a decline to $16.9 million from $24.2 million.

Geographical Performance: The U.S. segment experienced a significant decline in net revenues, attributed to the termination of the Business Relationship Agreement with McDonald’s USA and the closure of lower-volume DFD Doors. In contrast, the International segment showed resilience with a slight increase in revenues, driven by strong performance in markets like Canada and France.

Sales Units: Krispy Kreme added 131 net new Global Points of Access in the second quarter of fiscal 2025, reaching a total of 18,113. This includes 2,102 Krispy Kreme branded shops and 15,964 DFD Doors, reflecting the company's ongoing expansion efforts.

New Product Launches: During the second quarter, Krispy Kreme introduced specialty doughnut offerings and seasonal activations, including collaborations with PAC-MAN, Mother’s Day, and Easter themes, enhancing consumer engagement and brand visibility.

New Production Launches: The company opened its first franchise shop in Brazil, marking a significant step in its strategy to increase global presence and expand into new markets.

Future Outlook: Krispy Kreme plans to open in three to four new countries in fiscal 2025, with a focus on increasing the percentage of revenues and Adjusted EBITDA generated outside the U.S. The company is also evaluating opportunities to refranchise certain equity markets to support its capital-light franchising strategy.

SEC Filing: Krispy Kreme, Inc. [ DNUT ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: HERITAGE COMMERCE CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Heritage Commerce Corp, a financial services company operating primarily in California, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting both the challenges and opportunities faced during the period.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest Income: Heritage Commerce Corp reported a total interest income of $63.025 million, marking an increase from $58.489 million in the same period last year. This growth was driven by higher interest income from loans and securities.

Net Interest Income Before Provision for Credit Losses on Loans: The company saw an improvement in its net interest income before provision for credit losses on loans, which rose to $44.805 million from $38.867 million, indicating improved interest margins.

Net Income: Net income for the period was $6.389 million, a decrease from $9.234 million in the prior year. This decline was primarily attributed to higher noninterest expenses.

Basic Earnings Per Share (EPS): Basic EPS stood at $0.10, compared to $0.15 in the previous year, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Diluted EPS: Diluted EPS was consistent with the basic earnings per share at $0.10, indicating no significant dilution from potential common shares.

Business Highlights

Segment Information: Heritage Commerce Corp operates through two primary segments: banking and factoring. The banking segment serves clients primarily in California, while the factoring segment, through Bay View Funding, provides working capital financing across the United States.

Geographical Performance: The company's banking operations are concentrated in several counties in California, including Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, San Benito, San Francisco, San Mateo, and Santa Clara. This regional focus allows Heritage Commerce Corp to leverage local market knowledge and relationships.

Operational Performance: Heritage Commerce Corp has maintained a strong loan portfolio with a focus on commercial real estate, both owner-occupied and non-owner occupied, as well as residential mortgages and multifamily loans. The allowance for credit losses on loans is carefully managed to reflect the credit risk characteristics of the portfolio.

Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in both the banking and factoring segments, with a focus on expanding customer relationships and maintaining credit quality. The company is also monitoring economic conditions and regulatory changes that could impact future operations.

SEC Filing: HERITAGE COMMERCE CORP [ HTBK ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc., a leading provider of financial services primarily focused on the legal and litigation market, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities for the three months ended June 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc. reported strong financial performance for the second quarter of 2025, showcasing significant growth across key metrics:

  • Total Interest Income: $33.5 million, up from $27.4 million in the same period of 2024, driven by higher interest income from loans and securities.
  • Net Interest Income: $29.3 million, an increase from $24.3 million in the same period of 2024, indicating improved interest margins.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $3.5 million, compared to $1.0 million in the same period of 2024, reflecting adjustments for potential credit risks.
  • Net Income: $11.9 million, up from $10.5 million in the same period of 2024, showing positive growth in profitability.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share (EPS): $1.48, compared to $1.34 in the same period of 2024, indicating an increase in earnings per share.
  • Diluted EPS: $1.38, up from $1.25 in the same period of 2024, reflecting improved earnings performance.
  • Total Noninterest Income: $6.6 million, slightly up from $6.3 million in the same period of 2024, driven by stable payment processing fees and other income sources.
  • Total Noninterest Expense: $17.1 million, compared to $15.2 million in the same period of 2024, primarily due to increased employee compensation and benefits.
  • Net Income Before Income Taxes: $15.2 million, compared to $14.4 million in the same period of 2024, indicating a strong pre-tax income performance.

Business Highlights

Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc. also provided insights into its business operations and future outlook:

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported noninterest income primarily from payment processing fees, administrative service income, and customer-related fees. Payment processing fees, which include payment processing income and ACH income, are significant contributors to noninterest income.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in its legal and litigation market lending and aims to capitalize on growth opportunities in new markets. The company is also focused on managing operations under current economic conditions and mitigating risks associated with its loan and investment portfolios.

SEC Filing: Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc. [ ESQ ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: DigitalBridge Group, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
DigitalBridge Group, Inc., a leading global investment manager specializing in digital infrastructure, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial and operational changes, reflecting the company's ongoing transformation and strategic focus on digital infrastructure investments.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: The company reported total revenues of $(3.207) million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, a stark contrast to the $390.336 million reported for the same period in 2024. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, total revenues were $42.240 million, compared to $464.729 million for the same period in 2024. This significant decrease is primarily attributed to negative carried interest allocation.

Net Income (Loss): DigitalBridge Group, Inc. experienced a net loss of $(25.684) million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a net income of $129.928 million for the same period in 2024. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, the net loss was $(40.539) million, compared to a net income of $99.162 million for the same period in 2024.

Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Common Stockholders: The net income attributable to common stockholders was $16.962 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, down from $76.763 million for the same period in 2024. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, the net income attributable to common stockholders was $16.084 million, compared to $32.475 million for the same period in 2024.

Net Income (Loss) Per Share—Basic: The basic net income per share was $0.10 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.44 for the same period in 2024. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, the basic net income per share was $0.09, compared to $0.19 for the same period in 2024.

Net Income (Loss) Per Share—Diluted: The diluted net income per share was $0.10 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.44 for the same period in 2024. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, the diluted net income per share was $0.09, compared to $0.19 for the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The primary source of fee revenue for DigitalBridge Group, Inc. comes from management fees, which are calculated based on various contractual rates for different fund types, including commingled equity funds, credit and other equity funds, co-investment vehicles, and liquid strategy funds. Additionally, incentive fees are earned from sub-advisory accounts in the liquid securities strategy, determined by the performance of the respective accounts and subject to specified return thresholds. Other fees include advisory fees, loan origination fees from co-investors, and service fees for IT, facilities, and operational support provided to certain portfolio companies.

Future Outlook: DigitalBridge Group, Inc. continues to focus on its transformation into a leading global investment manager in digital infrastructure. The company aims to expand its investment management platform and achieve growth in its flagship value-add digital infrastructure equity offerings.

SEC Filing: DigitalBridge Group, Inc. [ DBRG ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Braskem Confirms Talks With Unipar About a Possible Deal for Braskem U.S. Assets
89bio, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
89bio, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative therapies for liver and cardio-metabolic diseases, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2023. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and significant business developments, highlighting its ongoing commitment to advancing its clinical programs and preparing for future growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Loss from operations: $(115.8) million, reflecting increased research and development expenses primarily due to higher contract manufacturing and clinical development costs.
  • Net loss: $(111.5) million, driven by increased operating expenses and interest expense, partially offset by interest income.
  • Net loss per share, basic and diluted: $(0.71), reflecting the impact of increased net loss and higher weighted-average shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Pegozafermin MASH Program: The company is advancing pegozafermin through Phase 3 clinical development for the treatment of metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH). The ENLIGHTEN program includes two global, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trials: ENLIGHTEN-Fibrosis and ENLIGHTEN-Cirrhosis, with anticipated topline data in 2027 and 2028, respectively.
  • Pegozafermin SHTG Program: Pegozafermin is also being developed for severe hypertriglyceridemia (SHTG). The Phase 3 ENTRUST trial completed enrollment in December 2024, with topline data expected in the first quarter of 2026.
  • Manufacturing and Commercial Supply Preparedness: A collaboration with BiBo Biopharma Engineering Co., Ltd. is underway to construct a production facility in China for pegozafermin's commercial supply. The facility is expected to meet projected commercial needs, with a total contractual obligation of $175.0 million.
  • Clinical Development Progress: Significant progress in clinical trials, including the ENLIGHTEN and ENTRUST trials, reflects the company's strategic focus on advancing pegozafermin for MASH and SHTG.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued investment in research and development, with a focus on advancing pegozafermin through clinical trials and preparing for potential regulatory submissions.

SEC Filing: 89bio, Inc. [ ETNB ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Braskem and Unipar in Talks Regarding Potential Opportunities Involving Assets and/or Equity Interests of Braskem
US stocks weekly: Reheat
data.description:
** S&P 500 rallies 2.4% on Fed rate cut hopes

** Dow up 1.4%, while Nasdaq Composite advances 3.9%

** Week started out with traders assessing the recent damage, along with signs that momentum was coming up lame

** But then appeared the DJI setting up for another run at the wall, and by Fri, Nasdaq scores its 18th record closing high of 2025

** Despite new IXIC highs, sputtering thrust is a concern

** Still, traders pose a question: "Mirror, mirror on the wall, will small caps ever be the fairest of them all?"

** U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield , holds 4.187% support; rises back toward ~4.30%

** Most sectors steam ahead; Tech and Consumer Discretionary on the front burner, while just Energy and Healthcare and Real Estate are left in the freezer

** Tech leaps 4.2%. Palantir surges >20% after second annual rev forecast hike this year on surging AI demand

Apple shares jump >13% as $100 bln U.S. manufacturing pledge eases tariff fears

Chipmakers climb as tariff impact seen as contained

Semiconductor index gains 2.7%

** Consumer Discretionary rallies 3.7%. McDonald's rises as global sales exceed expectations, aided by affordable meal options

** Communication Services up 3.3%. Walt Disney tops earnings forecasts with streaming gains, and raises guidance; nonetheless, shares fall Weds, end down >3% for week

** Industrials up 0.6%. TASER-maker Axon Enterprise fires higher to record highs after Q2 beat, boosted 2025 rev forecast, rallies >13% for week

** Healthcare sags 0.8%. Eli Lilly tumbles after oral weight-loss drug data fails to impress. Though LLY pill data 'fell short, but not that short', BofA analysts say. LLY sheds ~18% for week

Vertex Pharmaceuticals slumps after pain drug setback. VRTX down ~21% for week

** Meanwhile, big bets on a September rate cut don't mean it'll actually happen, and individual investor bears break out`

** SPX performance YTD:

Tech

15.6%

Comm Svcs

15.0%

Industrials

14.3%

Utils

13.6%

data.title: Lument Finance Trust Reports Second Quarter 2025 Results
data.description:
NEW YORK, Aug. 8, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Lument Finance Trust, Inc. ("we", "LFT" or "the Company") today reported its second quarter results. GAAP net income attributable to common shareholders for the second quarter was $2.5 million, or $0.05 per share of common stock. Distributable earnings for the second quarter were $2.8 million, or $0.05 per share of common stock. The Company has also issued a detailed presentation of its results, which can be viewed at www.lumentfinancetrust.com.

Conference Call and Webcast Information

The Company will also host a conference call on Monday, August 11, 2025, at 8:30 a.m. ET to provide a business update and discuss the financial results for the second quarter of 2025. The conference call may be accessed by dialing 1-800-836-8184 (U.S.) or 1-646-357-8785 (international). Note: there is no passcode; please ask the operator to be joined into the Lument Finance Trust call. A live webcast, on a listen-only basis, is also available and can be accessed through the URL:

https://app.webinar.net/pGm1xAzPEMq

For those unable to listen to the live broadcast, a recorded replay will be available for on-demand viewing approximately one hour after the end of the event through the Company's website https://lumentfinancetrust.com/ and by telephone dial-in. The replay call-in number is 1-888-660-6345 (U.S.) or 1-646-517-4150 (international) with passcode 48892.

Non-GAAP Financial Measures

In this release, the Company presents certain financial measures that are not calculated according to generally accepted accounting principles in the United States ("GAAP"). Specifically, the Company is presenting distributable earnings, which constitutes a non-GAAP financial measure within the meaning of Item 10(e) of Regulation S-K and is net income under GAAP. While we believe the non-GAAP information included in this press release provides supplemental information to assist investors in analyzing our results, and to assist investors in comparing our results with other peer issuers, these measures are not in accordance with GAAP, and they should not be considered a substitute for, or superior to, our financial information calculated in accordance with GAAP. The methods of calculating non-GAAP financial measures may differ substantially from similarly titled measures used by other companies. Our GAAP financial results and the reconciliations from these results should be carefully evaluated.

Distributable Earnings

Distributable Earnings is a non-GAAP measure, which we define as GAAP net income (loss) attributable to holders of common stock computed in accordance with GAAP, including realized losses not otherwise included in GAAP net income (loss) and excluding (i) non-cash equity compensation, (ii) depreciation and amortization, (iii) any unrealized gains or losses or other similar non-cash items that are included in net income for that applicable reporting period, regardless of whether such items are included in other comprehensive income (loss) or net income (loss), and (iv) one-time events pursuant to changes in GAAP and certain material non-cash income or expense items after discussions with the Company's Board of Directors and approved by a majority of the Company's independent directors. Distributable Earnings mirrors how we calculate "Core Earnings" pursuant to the terms of our management agreement with our manager, Lument Investment Management, LLC ("Manager"), for purposes of calculating the incentive fee payable to our Manager.

While Distributable Earnings excludes the impact of any unrealized provisions for credit losses, any loan losses are charged off and realized through Distributable Earnings when deemed non-recoverable. Non-recoverability is determined (i) upon the resolution of a loan (i.e. when the loan is repaid, fully or partially, or in the case of foreclosures, when the underlying asset is sold), or (ii) with respect to any amount due under any loan, when such amount is determined to be non-collectible.

We believe that Distributable Earnings provides meaningful information to consider in addition to our net income (loss) and cash flows from operating activities determined in accordance with GAAP. We believe Distributable Earnings is a useful financial metric for existing and potential future holders of our common stock as historically, over time, Distributable Earnings has been a strong indicator of our dividends per share of common stock. As a REIT, we generally must distribute annually at least 90% of our taxable income, subject to certain adjustments, and therefore we believe our dividends are one of the principal reasons stockholders may invest in our common stock. Furthermore, Distributable Earnings help us to evaluate our performance excluding the effects of certain transactions and GAAP adjustments that we believe are not necessarily indicative of our current loan portfolio and operations and is a performance metric we consider when declaring our dividends.

Distributable Earnings does not represent net income (loss) or cash generated from operating activities and should not be considered as an alternative to GAAP net income (loss), or an indication of GAAP cash flows from operations, a measure of our liquidity, or an indication of funds available for our cash needs.

GAAP to Distributable Earnings Reconciliation

About LFT

LFT is a Maryland corporation focused on investing in, financing and managing a portfolio of commercial real estate debt investments. The Company primarily invests in transitional floating rate commercial mortgage loans with an emphasis on middle-market multi-family assets.

LFT is externally managed and advised by Lument Investment Management LLC, a Delaware limited liability company.

Additional Information and Where to Find It

Investors, security holders and other interested persons may find additional information regarding the Company at the SEC's Internet site at http://www.sec.gov/, the Company website at www.lumentfinancetrust.com, or by directing requests to: Lument Finance Trust, 230 Park Avenue, 20th Floor, New York, NY 10169, Attention: Investor Relations.

Forward-Looking Statements

Certain statements included in this press release constitute forward-looking statements intended to qualify for the safe harbor contained in Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act, as amended. Forward-looking statements are subject to risks and uncertainties. You can identify forward-looking statements by use of words such as "believe," "expect," "anticipate," "project," "estimate," "plan," "continue," "intend," "should," "may," "will," "seek," "would," "could," or similar expressions or other comparable terms, or by discussions of strategy, plans or intentions. Forward-looking statements are based on the Company's beliefs, assumptions and expectations of its future performance, taking into account all information currently available to the Company on the date of this press release or the date on which such statements are first made. Actual results may differ from expectations, estimates and projections. You are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements in this press release and should consider carefully the factors described in Part I, Item IA "Risk Factors" in the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024, which is available on the SEC's website at www.sec.gov, and in the Company's other current or periodic filings with the SEC, when evaluating these forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements are subject to substantial risks and uncertainties, many of which are difficult to predict and are generally beyond the Company's control. Except as required by applicable law, the Company disclaims any intention or obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/lument-finance-trust-reports-second-quarter-2025-results-302525586.html

SOURCE Lument Finance Trust, Inc.

data.title: US stocks close the week pointing higher
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes end higher; Nasdaq out front with ~1% gain
  • Tech leads S&P 500 sector gainers; Real Estate weakest group
  • Dollar rallies; gold edges red; bitcoin, crude dip
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield rises to ~4.29%

U.S. STOCKS CLOSE THE WEEK POINTING HIGHER

Wall Street's three major indexes finished higher on Friday with the Magnificent 7 pulling the heaviest weight in the rally. The Nasdaq composite registered its second straight record closing high, marking its 18th finishing record this year.

In the also-ran department, the S&P 500 really tried but just couldn't squeak past its July 28 closing level in the end, missing out by just 0.16 points. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose on the day, but ended down a timid 1.86% below its last record close, which was on Dec. 4.

Apple was by far the biggest boost to the S&P 500 on Friday as the stock still appeared to be dining out on a pledge the company made on Wednesday to invest an additional $100 billion in the U.S., potentially allowing it to sidestep tariffs on iPhones. Chief Executive Tim Cook also gifted U.S. President Trump a plaque with a 24-karat gold base. Apple rose 4.2% on the day and 13.3% for the week, marking its biggest weekly gain since 2020.

Speaking of the week, the S&P 500 added 2.4%, while the Nasdaq rose 3.9% and the Dow added 1.3%.

While investors spent the week pinning their hopes onto a more dovish Federal Reserve, the general outlook for the earnings season also improved during the first full trading week of August.

While the latest earnings were a mixed bag, the second-quarter blended growth rate, which includes estimates as well as actual reports, rose to 13.2%, compared with an 11.2% rate just a week ago, according to the latest data from LSEG. All of the S&P 500's 11 major sectors saw some improvement in their Q2 growth outlook during the week. Some of the heftiest improvements were in energy , now expected to report an earnings decline of 13.5% versus estimates for a 21% drop on August 1. Communication services is now expected to show a 49% growth rate up from 43.5% just a week ago.

Don't get too comfortable in your beach chair though. The monthly U.S. consumer price index report, due on Tuesday of next week, may test the market's rally, with some investors saying equities are primed for a potential pullback after rocketing to records.

Here is your closing snapshot for Friday:

(Sinéad Carew)

*****

EARLIER ON LIVE MARKETS:

FIVE REASONS THE ECONOMY MAY NOT ACTUALLY BE FINE CLICK HERE

INDIVIDUAL INVESTOR BEARS STAGE A COMEBACK - AAII CLICK HERE

GOLDMAN SACHS EYES EM RATE RALLY AS FED CUTS COME INTO VIEW CLICK HERE

GLOBAL EQUITY ALLOCATIONS AT PEAK SINCE FINANCIAL CRISIS CLICK HERE

data.title: Paragon Technologies News Release
data.description:
To the Shareholders of Paragon Technologies:

EASTON, PA / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / It has been a busy few weeks since the conclusion of the annual meeting as we are hard at work restoring Paragon Technologies, Inc. (OTCID:PGNT) ('Paragon' or the 'Company') and I realize many of you have lots of questions. The purpose of this letter is to provide a brief overview of where our Company sits today given the events that have transpired over the past months.

Thanks to an overwhelming show of support, our slate was elected with a supermajority of the vote - a strong mandate for change, and for stewardship that reflects the values of ownership, discipline, and integrity.

Now that the campaign is behind us, Paragon is back again focused on doing the simple things that made the Company an enormous success for over a decade: delivering value to our clients, operating efficiently with a disciplined approach to expenditure, avoiding waste and bureaucracy, behaving with candor, being faithful stewards, and acting opportunistically.

For the past nine months, former directors Samuel Weiser, Tim Eriksen, Howard Brownstein and David Lontini, a group of directors who had no real economic interest in Paragon, were focused solely on perpetuating themselves in control and wasting millions of dollars of Paragon's capital to achieve that goal. We have now learned what we suspected all along: mindless tactics led to mindless waste. Based on an internal review, we have learned the following and feel our shareholders should be aware of these findings so shareholders are clearly aware of the substantial financial impact they will have on our upcoming financial results:

  • Approximately $3.7 million spent on their entrenchment schemes.

  • Another $1.2-$1.4 million in estimated remaining liabilities related to the prior Board decisions including passage of the poison pill, manipulating the Company's bylaws, and the pursuit of a costly and selfish proxy contest against significant shareholder support - rather than engage in a resolution, they wasted shareholder money. But these are the choices often made when management has no real economic stake in a business.

  • The liquidation of over $1 million of Paragon's marketable securities in April and May when markets were volatile, selling securities during market lows resulting in meaningful realized losses for Paragon. Today those securities would have appreciated meaningfully.

  • The quick sale of real estate assets without proper board approval on hastily negotiated terms that likely left $100,000 - $200,000 on the table.

  • Mismanagement of SI Systems, leading to a significant increase in operating costs without achieving any meaningful revenue gains.

Prior to my removal as CEO, all three of Paragon's subsidiaries were profitable and Paragon's assets included nearly $5 million in cash and marketable securities, net of expenses associated with our previous corporate campaign. Today that figure sits just above $1 million. Some of the wasted resources may be recouped, but the timing and likelihood of any recoupment is uncertain at this time.

Certainly, these expenditures will have a significant financial impact on Paragon and its financial results for the remainder of the year as we clear out the debris left behind. However, this episode also reveals Paragon's underlying strength and resiliency - resiliency that was painstakingly built over years of stewardship by management that was economically aligned with stockholders. We will do it again.

Protecting the downside is the core of our philosophy at Paragon. By prioritizing protecting stockholder capital, we are prioritizing our commitment to enhancing all stakeholder value. Our customers and other partners can rest assured Paragon will continue to be committed to providing them with value in our products and we will serve you with the utmost reliability, quality, and service.

And that brings us to the future-where our energy is now fully focused. Our mood and outlook are positive. The right leaders are back at the Company, and we have quickly acted and are seeing some results. SEDC just posted its highest monthly sales of the year in July, exceeding $13 million. We are already hard at work executing on the plan we laid out to shareholders: re-engaging with our partners, setting the initial steps for the expansion of our distribution and automation businesses and cutting unnecessary costs. We are focusing on long-term value while also prioritizing the need to minimize future financial loss in the short term.

To the vast majority of shareholders who stood with us through this process, we thank you. We can never promise perfection, but you can once again rely on us to communicate candidly, conduct our business affairs with prudence and integrity, and make decisions through the lens of owners - a recipe that has produced a decade of tremendous achievement for Paragon. At Paragon, our vision has always been to build something that lasts - and nothing will shake our confidence in doing just that. We have some rebuilding to do and that will take time, but the Company again has management 100% economically aligned with stockholders. We will act boldly and opportunistically, but most of all carefully.

The 2025 annual letter to shareholders is forthcoming (that streak will not be broken!) and I look forward to providing a more detailed overview of Paragon towards the end of the third quarter.

Sincerely

Sham Gad

CEO

Paragon Technologies, Inc.

CONTACT:

ir@pgntgroup.com

SOURCE: Paragon Technologies Inc.

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: FULTON FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Fulton Financial Corp, a prominent player in the financial services industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and business operations, showcasing notable growth and strategic advancements.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $402.8 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year due to higher income from investment securities.
  • Net Interest Income: $254.9 million, an increase from the previous year, driven by a reduction in interest expense.
  • Net Income: $99.2 million, showing growth compared to the previous year, supported by lower non-interest expenses.
  • Net Income Available to Common Shareholders (basic): $96.6 million, with a consistent per-share value of $0.53 compared to the previous year.
  • Net Income Available to Common Shareholders (diluted): $0.53 per share, slightly higher than the previous year's $0.52, indicating improved profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported revenue from various segments including wealth management, commercial banking, consumer banking, and mortgage banking. Wealth management and commercial banking showed growth compared to the previous year, indicating strong performance in these areas.
  • Geographical Performance: The acquisition of Republic First Bank enhanced the company's presence in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey, suggesting a strategic focus on expanding market reach in these regions.
  • Sales Units: The company originated $236.7 million in mortgage loans held for sale during the six months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting active participation in the mortgage market.
  • Future Outlook: Management expects continued growth in the wealth management and commercial banking segments, supported by strategic acquisitions and market expansion efforts. The company is also focused on maintaining strong asset quality and managing credit risk effectively.

SEC Filing: FULTON FINANCIAL CORP [ FULT ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: FIRST NORTHERN COMMUNITY BANCORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
First Northern Community Bancorp, a prominent banking institution operating primarily in the Sacramento Valley region of Northern California, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, showcasing significant growth in key financial metrics and providing insights into its business operations and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Interest and Dividend Income: The company reported $20.574 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting an increase from $19.530 million for the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by higher income from loans and taxable investment securities.

Net Interest Income: Net interest income rose to $16.953 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $15.978 million for the same period in 2024, indicating improved interest income performance.

Net Income: The company achieved a net income of $5.466 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $4.424 million for the same period in 2024, showcasing a positive growth in profitability.

Basic EPS: Basic earnings per share (EPS) increased to $0.35 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $0.28 for the same period in 2024, reflecting increased earnings per share.

Diluted EPS: Diluted EPS was $0.35 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $0.27 for the same period in 2024, indicating improved earnings performance on a diluted basis.

Business Highlights

Geographical Performance: The company operates primarily in the Sacramento Valley region of Northern California, where it derives most of its revenues from lending and deposit-taking activities. The business environment in this region, including interest rates, business conditions, and customer confidence, significantly impacts the company's ability to generate revenues.

Segment Information: First Northern Community Bancorp operates as a single reportable segment, focusing on traditional banking operations. This includes gathering deposits and originating loans, which are the primary sources of revenue.

Loan Portfolio Composition: The company's loan portfolio includes commercial, commercial real estate, agriculture, residential mortgage, residential construction, and consumer loans. The composition and risk grading of these loans are critical to the company's operational performance.

Credit Quality and Risk Management: The company actively manages credit quality and asset quality, with a focus on maintaining a robust allowance for credit losses. This involves assessing economic conditions and trends, credit cycles, and the impact of tariffs on imported goods.

Operational Efficiency: The company continues to focus on operational efficiency, with significant expenses arising from interest expense, provisions for credit losses, salaries and employee benefits, occupancy and equipment, and data processing.

Future Outlook: Management anticipates that interest rates, regulatory changes, and economic conditions will continue to influence the company's operations. The company is also focused on managing its interest rate risk profile and expects changes in residential mortgage interest rates to impact new originations and refinancing activities.

SEC Filing: FIRST NORTHERN COMMUNITY BANCORP [ FNRN ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025

data.title: Douglas Emmett Inc SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Douglas Emmett Inc, a leading real estate investment trust (REIT) specializing in office and multifamily properties, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, highlighting key metrics and strategic initiatives that have shaped its recent performance.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $252.4 million. The increase was primarily due to rental revenues and tenant recoveries from a joint venture (JV) consolidated on January 1, 2025, and an office property acquired in January 2025.

Net (Loss) Income: $(15.1) million. The decrease was primarily due to higher interest expenses and operating expenses.

Net (Loss) Income Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(5.8) million. The decrease was primarily due to higher interest expenses and operating expenses.

Net (Loss) Income Per Common Share – Basic and Diluted: $(0.04). Reflects the impact of higher interest expenses and operating expenses.

Operating Expenses: $206.8 million. The increase was primarily due to higher property taxes, rental expenses from a JV consolidated on January 1, 2025, and an office property acquired in January 2025.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

During the six months ended June 30, 2025, revenues from the Total Portfolio were derived from office and multifamily segments. The office segment generated $404.9 million, while the multifamily segment contributed $99.1 million. The office segment's revenue increase was driven by rental revenues and tenant recoveries from a newly consolidated JV and an office property acquired in January 2025.

Geographical Performance

Douglas Emmett, Inc. operates primarily in Los Angeles County, California, and Honolulu, Hawaii. The company's performance is influenced by economic and regulatory developments in these regions. The consolidation of Partnership X, which owns properties in the Los Angeles submarkets of Brentwood and Beverly Hills, has impacted regional performance.

Sales Units

The In-Service Portfolio includes 69 office properties with 17.5 million rentable square feet and 13 multifamily properties with 4,410 units. The Development Portfolio consists of two office properties and one multifamily property, with ongoing development activities.

New Product Launches

The company is planning a 320-unit apartment community at 10900 Wilshire Boulevard in Westwood. This involves converting an existing 247,000 square foot office tower into apartments and integrating it with a new residential building.

New Production Launches

The redevelopment of Studio Plaza in Burbank is underway, converting it from a single-tenant to a multi-tenant office building. This project is part of the company's strategy to reposition properties for optimal use and tenant mix.

Future Outlook

The company anticipates continued development and repositioning activities, including the conversion of 10900 Wilshire Boulevard and the redevelopment of Studio Plaza. These projects are expected to enhance the company's portfolio and operational performance over the coming years.

SEC Filing: Douglas Emmett Inc [ DEI ] - 10-Q - Aug. 08, 2025
